sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Fulham are struggling in 17th place with 8 points, having won only 2 of their last 5 matches, with a recent draw and three losses. Wolverhampton are in a dire situation at the bottom of the table with just 2 points, winless in their last 5 matches, including three consecutive losses. Both teams have failed to keep a clean sheet in their recent games, indicating defensive vulnerabilities.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two head-to-head encounters, results are split with Fulham winning 2-1 away at Wolverhampton in February 2025, while Wolves secured a convincing 4-1 victory at Craven Cottage in November 2024. Notably, both teams have scored in their last 5 meetings, and matches often produce over 2.5 goals, suggesting an open game.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Fulham have scored 9 goals this season (13th in the league) but concede regularly (14 goals, 11th rank), with a decent big chance conversion rate of 42.9%. Wolverhampton’s attack is weaker with just 7 goals (one of the lowest), while their defense has leaked 19 goals, the second-worst in the league. Fulham’s superior possession (48.8%) and passing accuracy (82.5%) could give them an edge over Wolves.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Trends
sections.text: Alex Iwobi stands out for Fulham against Wolves, averaging a 7.56 Sofascore rating with 3 goals in their last 5 matchups. Both teams struggle defensively, with no clean sheets in their last 5 games each, and head-to-head trends show both teams scoring consistently. Fulham’s higher foul count (127, most in the league) might lead to cards.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Fulham’s slight edge in form, league position, and Sofascore’s predicted 53% chance of a home win, they are favored to win. Wolverhampton’s poor away form and defensive frailty make an upset unlikely, though their scoring in recent head-to-heads suggests they might nick a goal. Expect a competitive match with goals on both sides.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half at Craven Cottage saw Fulham take a narrow lead over Wolverhampton with a 1-0 scoreline. Fulham dominated possession and controlled much of the game, creating more entries into the final third and penalty area compared to their opponents. Ryan Sessegnon’s early goal in the 9th minute proved to be the difference, giving Fulham the edge. Wolverhampton, however, struggled to create clear-cut chances, and their situation worsened with a red card that left them a man down for the remainder of the match.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Fulham's attacking intent
halftimeSections.text: Fulham displayed a strong attacking presence throughout the first half. They capitalized on their early momentum to score through Sessegnon, with consistent pressure in the opposition half. Their ability to create opportunities was evident with multiple corner kicks and a big chance created. Players like Alex Iwobi and Raúl Jiménez played key roles in linking play and setting up dangerous moves. Fulham will likely look to exploit their numerical advantage in the second half to extend their lead.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Wolverhampton's defensive challenges
halftimeSections.text: Wolverhampton faced significant challenges in the first half, particularly after being reduced to ten men. Their defensive structure was tested repeatedly, and while they managed to limit Fulham to just one goal, their lack of possession and fewer entries into dangerous areas hampered their ability to respond. Despite having a couple of shots on target, they couldn’t convert their limited opportunities. The red card to Emmanuel Agbadou has left them vulnerable, and they’ll need a monumental effort to avoid conceding further.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half saw distinct shifts between the two sides. Wolverhampton started with some early intent, pushing forward in the opening minutes with a few attacking spells. However, Fulham quickly took control around the time of their goal and maintained a sustained period of dominance with consistent attacking waves. While Wolverhampton had brief moments of resurgence, particularly towards the end of the half, Fulham largely dictated the tempo and looked more threatening in their forward movements.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Fulham, Ryan Sessegnon stands out after his first-half goal and will be a key figure in their push for more in the second half. Raúl Jiménez, with his assist and involvement in creating big chances, could also be pivotal. On the Wolverhampton side, Jean-Ricner Bellegarde showed some promise with his shot on target and could be their best hope for a response, though their numerical disadvantage will make it tough. Wolverhampton will need a disciplined performance from their defensive line, including Santiago Bueno, to keep the scoreline tight.
